data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-16T01:43:22.355Z</current_datetime>

I have this MSTest data-driven test using DynamicData with object arrays. Is there a more type-safe way to write this in modern MSTest?

```csharp
[TestMethod]
[DynamicData(nameof(TestData))]
public void Validate_ReturnsExpected(string input, bool expected)
{
    var result = Validator.IsValid(input);
    Assert.AreEqual(expected, result);
}

public static IEnumerable<object[]> TestData =>
[
    ["valid@email.com", true],
    ["invalid", false],
    ["", false],
];
```

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a more type-safe way to write data-driven tests in modern MSTest, specifically replacing `DynamicData` with `object[]` arrays. In MSTest v3+, there's a feature called `[DataRow]` with typed parameters, but the real type-safe improvement in modern MSTest (v3.8+/v4) is the generic `DynamicData<T>` or using tuples with `[DynamicData]`. Let me think about what's available.

Actually, in MSTest v3.6+, they introduced `[DynamicData]` with generic tuple support, allowing you to use `IEnumerable<(T1, T2, ...)>` instead of `IEnumerable<object[]>`. 

In the more recent versions, MSTest v3.8+ and v4 added the fully generic `[DynamicData<T1, T2, ...>]` attribute, which gives you complete type safety without needing to cast or work with object arrays at all.
